macro_events.event_summary: Fed funds futures have flipped to pricing the next Fed move as a hike rather than a cut, following a hot inflation print.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer / rising front-end rates lifting NIM on floating-rate assets
Higher discount rate compressing long-duration equity multiples
Stronger USD pressuring commodity prices and multinational translation
Inflation persistence favoring real-asset and pricing-power names

beneficiaries.rationale: Schwab's earnings are highly geared to short rates via client cash sweep yields and reduced need for supplemental funding; a hike repricing directly lifts net interest revenue and removes the bank-deposit-flight overhang.
Interactive Brokers earns net interest on ~$100B+ of customer cash and margin balances; every 25bp of additional Fed tightening flows almost directly to pretax income with minimal deposit beta.
Moody's and ratings peers are not direct beneficiaries — replacing with better fit.
Allstate and P&C insurers benefit from higher reinvestment yields on float portfolios; a hawkish repricing extends the tailwind to investment income that has been a key earnings driver.
CME Group sees direct volume uplift in SOFR/Fed Funds futures and Treasury complex when rate path is repriced; hawkish surprises historically produce record interest-rate ADV days.

losers.rationale: D.R. Horton and homebuilders face direct demand destruction as 30-year mortgage rates reprice higher off the back of a hawkish Fed path; affordability is already at multi-decade lows.
Zillow's revenue is levered to housing transaction volumes, which collapse when mortgage rates spike; second-order hit on top of the homebuilder demand shock.
High-multiple, long-duration software names with rich forward P/Es (PLTR ~70x+) see the largest DCF impact from discount-rate increases; positioning is also crowded which amplifies the unwind.
Realty Income and triple-net REITs trade as bond proxies with high duration; hawkish repricing widens the cap-rate vs. Treasury spread and pressures NAV.

headlines: Traders now see next Fed interest rate move as a hike following inflation surge
